intent: Load the DeepSeek-V3 golden fixture, sweep sequence length from 2K to 32K, and watch the KV cache bar dominate the stacked VRAM chart.

steps.blind_description: Terminal showing monospaced output with a block of numeric columns. (frame slug: landing)
steps.intent: Planner loaded with the default fixture; sidebar exposes seq length, concurrent users, and quantisation controls.

steps.blind_description: Command-line prompt with recently-emitted lines of hex-coloured log output. (frame slug: sidebar-open)
steps.intent: Sidebar expanded, showing the sequence length slider at its default 4096 tokens.

steps.blind_description: Dark terminal displaying a table of CLI results and a blinking cursor at the bottom. (frame slug: seq-bumped)
steps.intent: Sequence length bumped upward; the stacked VRAM chart re-renders with a taller KV cache band.

steps.blind_description: Shell session showing a command header and a paragraph of structured stdout. (frame slug: layer-heatmap)
steps.intent: Per-layer KV contribution heatmap visible below the main chart; deeper layers contribute more memory.

steps.blind_description: Terminal window with a list-style summary and no visible error indicator. (frame slug: export-row)
steps.intent: Export row in view: vLLM, llama.cpp, and MLX buttons ready to emit deploy-ready configs for the current plan.
